skill.name: azure-eventgrid-py
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure Event Grid SDK for Python to publish CloudEvents, EventGridEvents, and namespace topic events.
skill.summary: Build Python publishers for Azure Event Grid with CloudEvents and EventGridEvent examples.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline backticks, placeholder Azure URLs, and an ellipsis in an Azure resource path. I found no executable skill scripts, no prompt injection text, no credential collection, and no evidence of data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Example
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown examples, mainly a package installation command and Python snippets. They are not executed by the skill and do not include user-controlled shell interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only shell command is inside a fenced documentation block. No script file or executable hook is present.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Azure Endpoint URLs
low_findings.description: The reported hardcoded URLs are placeholder Azure Event Grid endpoints used to explain configuration. They are not third-party collection endpoints and no request code sends secrets to them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs contain angle-bracket placeholders for Azure resources. They document normal SDK configuration rather than directing traffic to an attacker-controlled host.

low_findings.title: Azure Resource Path Placeholder
low_findings.description: The reported path traversal sequence is an ellipsis in an Azure resource identifier example. It does not form filesystem access and cannot traverse local paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The string is shown as an Azure topic resource path inside a Python object example. There is no filesystem API usage in the skill.

low_findings.title: Scanner Keyword Matches Without Security Impact
low_findings.description: The reported blocker and reconnaissance matches are caused by ordinary Event Grid documentation terms and imports. No weak cryptography, system probing, or host inspection behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found descriptive SDK guidance, not cryptographic primitives or reconnaissance commands. The flagged lines do not instruct host enumeration.

content.value_statement: Python developers often need clear examples for publishing Event Grid messages without mixing schemas or clients. This skill provides focused Azure SDK guidance for CloudEvents, EventGridEvents, async publishing, and namespace topics.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install azure-eventgrid and azure-identity for Python projects.
Explains endpoint variables for custom topics and Event Grid namespaces.
Provides examples for publishing single and batched CloudEvents.
Shows how to create EventGridEvent payloads with required fields.
Covers asynchronous publishing with the aio client.
Describes namespace topic publishing with the namespace_topic argument.
limitations: It does not deploy Azure resources or create Event Grid topics.
It does not validate real Azure credentials, permissions, or endpoint names.
It does not cover subscriber handlers or webhook validation flows.
It provides examples, not a full production retry or observability framework.

use_cases.title: Add Event Publishing
use_cases.description: Create Python publisher code for a service that sends domain events to Azure Event Grid.

use_cases.title: Standardize Event Schemas
use_cases.description: Compare CloudEvents and Event Grid schema usage before choosing a message format.

use_cases.title: Prepare Async Pipelines
use_cases.description: Use async client patterns for high-throughput event producers and namespace topic publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Publisher
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a custom topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to publish one CloudEvent to Azure Event Grid from Python using DefaultAzureCredential.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Event Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare CloudEvent and EventGridEvent for my Python Event Grid publisher and recommend one for a new service.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Domain Events
prompt_templates.scenario: Publishing multiple events from one workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Python Event Grid publisher that batches order events and uses meaningful event types and subjects.

prompt_templates.title: Design Async Namespace Publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an async Python publishing pattern for Event Grid namespaces, including endpoint configuration and namespace_topic usage.

output_examples.input: I need to publish an order-created event from Python.
output_examples.output: A concise publisher outline using EventGridPublisherClient, DefaultAzureCredential, a CloudEvent type, a source path, and a structured order payload.

output_examples.input: I am not sure whether to use CloudEvents or EventGridEvent.
output_examples.output: A decision summary that recommends CloudEvents for new interoperable systems and EventGridEvent for Azure-native schema compatibility.
An async publishing approach with batched events, meaningful subjects, and Event Grid namespace topic guidance.

best_practices: Use CloudEvents for new systems that need portable event schemas.
Batch related events when throughput matters and payloads share publishing context.
Use clear event types and subjects so subscribers can filter events reliably.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode real credentials, keys, or tenant secrets in publisher code.
Do not mix CloudEvent and EventGridEvent fields in the same event object.
Do not publish vague event types that make routing and filtering difficult.

faq.question: Does this skill create Azure Event Grid resources?
faq.answer: No. It explains Python SDK publishing patterns for existing topics and namespaces.

faq.question: Which authentication method does it show?
faq.answer: It uses DefaultAzureCredential from azure-identity in the examples.

faq.question: Does it support CloudEvents?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends CloudEvents for new interoperable applications.

faq.question: Does it cover asynchronous publishing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes the aio EventGridPublisherClient pattern for async sends.

faq.question: Can it help with Event Grid namespaces?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows namespace endpoints and namespace_topic usage for namespace topics.

faq.question: Does it include subscriber or webhook handling?
faq.answer: No. The content focuses on publishing events from Python.
